title: EZDRM
subtitle: DRM as a Service for streaming

steps.label: What They Do
steps.body:
EZDRM coined the term DRMaaS (DRM as a Service) over 20 years ago. They issue licenses for Widevine, FairPlay, and PlayReady — the three DRM systems every streaming platform must support to satisfy studio licensing requirements.

No studio content (Netflix, Disney+, Max, Prime) ships without DRM. EZDRM handles the key servers and license issuance.

steps.label: How They Run on Akamai
steps.body:
EZDRM offers an Akamai Connected Cloud-native configuration. Their license servers run on Linode and LKE adjacent to edge encoders/packagers (Ateme, Bitmovin), so license requests don't round-trip across the public internet.

Result: lower-latency license issuance, which translates to faster player startup time.

steps.label: When To Position
steps.body: Any deal involving premium/studio content, paid SVOD, or live sports rights — DRM is non-negotiable. Position EZDRM alongside Ateme (encoder/packager) and Yospace (SSAI) for a full premium-streaming stack on Akamai.
"EZDRM = DRMaaS adjacent to your encoders. Faster license issuance = faster player startup."

quiz.question: Why does EZDRM run adjacent to edge encoders on Akamai?

options.text: Lower license-issuance latency = faster player startup
options.explain: License calls happen on every play/seek. Co-locating with the packager cuts the round-trip.

options.text: It's the only place Linodes are available
options.explain: Linodes are everywhere — proximity to the packager is the actual reason.

options.text: DRM keys must be stored on the encoder
options.explain: Keys live in the license server, not the encoder.
Linode pricing is flat across regions. Latency is the driver.

quiz.question: A customer is launching an SVOD service. Which three QCPs likely belong in the stack together?

options.explain: These are all valid QCPs but not the streaming trio.
Ateme encodes, Yospace inserts ads (if ad-supported), EZDRM protects with license issuance. The streaming trio.
MediaMelon is analytics, Scaleflex is DAM, Avesha is K8s — not the live streaming trio.
Ateme handles encoding, but you still need DRM for studio content.
